title: Required Reassessment Interval Exceeded Before Execution
scenario: A clinical action requires reassessment within a defined interval, but execution is attempted after that interval has elapsed.
initial_state: The action was admissible immediately after reassessment.
state_transition: The reassessment interval expired before execution.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Temporal admissibility requires a current reassessment basis when the action depends on recent clinical state.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the required reassessment is no longer temporally current.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when required reassessment timing has lapsed.
